task_completion.description: Open or edit the local Wazuh rules file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a text editor (like nano or vim) or a command (like cat or echo) interacting with /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml.

task_completion.description: Define the rule with the correct ID, level, timeframe, and frequency
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML tag for the rule includes the correct attributes, e.g., <rule id="100050" level="12" frequency="2" timeframe="60">.

task_completion.description: Set the parent rule ID to the SSH success rule
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML tag <if_sid>5715</if_sid> is visible within the rule definition.

task_completion.description: Include conditions for the same user and different source IPs
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML tags <same_user /> (or <same_user>) and <different_srcip /> (or <different_srcip>) are visible within the rule definition.

task_completion.description: Restart the Wazuh manager to apply the new rule
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command being executed to restart the service, such as 'systemctl restart wazuh-manager' or '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart'.

task_completion.description: Simulate the impossible travel attack by injecting logs
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing commands (e.g., using 'echo ... >>') appending at least two SSH 'Accepted password' logs for the same user ('travel_exec') but from different IP addresses to /var/log/auth.log.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually modified the rules file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses a text editor or shell redirection (>, >>) to write to /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml, rather than just printing the XML to the console.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually injected logs into the correct file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses shell redirection (>>) or a script to append the simulated log entries specifically to /var/log/auth.log.

integrity.description: Verify the agent restarted the Wazuh service after making changes
integrity.visual_evidence: A restart command for the Wazuh manager is visibly executed in the terminal after the rules file is saved.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The custom Wazuh rule must be added to /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml. It strictly requires rule_id='100050', level='12', timeframe='60', and frequency='2'. The parent rule must be '5715' (SSH success). The rule must contain conditions for the same user (<same_user />) and different source IPs (<different_srcip />). The attack simulation must inject logs into /var/log/auth.log.
